hero.title: IANLP 2026
hero.subtitle: 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing
hero.themeValue: Artificial Intelligence, Intelligent Computing and Digital Transformation for a Smarter Future
hero.description: The 1st international conference dedicated to advancing AI and NLP research, featuring Transformers, Large Language Models, RAG systems, and trustworthy language technologies. Join researchers and practitioners from around the world to share cutting-edge discoveries.
hero.quote: Gathering researchers, academics, and practitioners to share advances in NLP, Transformers, LLMs, and trustworthy AI.

topics.topic9: IoT, Edge Intelligence and Smart Environments
topics.topic11: AI for Education, Health, Industry and Society
topics.specialFocusDesc: Beyond the broad thematic areas above, IANLP 2026 highlights four research tracks where submissions are especially encouraged.
topics.coreNlpDesc: Classification, Named Entity Recognition, Parsing, Summarization, Machine Translation
topics.advancedModelsDesc: LLMs, Transformers, Prompting, Instruction Tuning, Semantic Search
topics.trustworthyAiDesc: Evaluation, Robustness, Bias Detection, Fairness, Explainability
topics.realWorldAppsDesc: Education, Healthcare, Legal Tech, Finance, Industry Applications

cfp.label: Call for Papers
cfp.title: Call for Papers
cfp.intro: IANLP 2026 invites original and unpublished research contributions in Artificial Intelligence for Natural Language Processing. Submissions must present novel scientific results and be written in English.
cfp.pub1: Peer-reviewed proceedings in a major series
cfp.req3: Full papers: 12-15+ pages; Short papers: 6-11 pages

dates.label: Schedule
dates.title: Important Dates & Agenda

submission.label: Submission
submission.title: Submission Guidelines
submission.subtitle: Everything you need to know about submitting your research to IANLP 2026

review.label: Review
review.title: Review Process
review.subtitle: IANLP 2026 applies a strict double-blind peer-review process (minimum 3 reviewers per paper)
review.accordion1Desc: Upon submission, papers undergo an initial desk review to verify they meet basic requirements (format, length, scope).
review.accordion2Desc: Papers are assigned to 3+ expert reviewers based on their expertise and conflict-of-interest declarations.
review.accordion3Desc: Concurrent with peer review, papers are screened for plagiarism using iThenticate or similar tools.
review.accordion4Desc: After reviews are compiled, a decision is made. Accepted papers must address reviewer comments.
review.accordion5Desc: Final papers are compiled into peer-reviewed proceedings. SPRINGER format and editorial requirements apply when applicable.

keynote.label: Speakers
keynote.title: We Invite Amazing Speakers
keynote.subtitle: Keynote speakers will be announced soon. IANLP 2026 will invite internationally recognized speakers in emerging topics such as:
keynote.invitedComingSoon: Invited speakers will be announced soon.
keynote.speaker1Bio: Jaouad DABOUNOU is Professor at Hassan 1st University (FST of Settat) since 1994. Doctorate in Modeling of Solids and Structures (UTC, France 1994). Leads research in Applied Mathematics and Artificial Intelligence, with a focus on AI applications in various domains. Is Director of the Artificial Intelligence Research and Applications (AIRA) laboratory. Coordinates projects on territorial information systems. Member of Institute of Iconomy (Paris). AI expert for the startup OneUp and ArcelorMittal. President of Association of Artificial Intelligence Development in Africa (AAIDA), and chairs the International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Advanced Topics and Societal Innovation (AIATSI).
keynote.speaker2Bio: Pepa Atanasova is Assistant Professor (Tenure Track) in the Department of Computer Science, NLP Section at the University of Copenhagen, and co-leads the CopeNLU group. Her research focuses on trustworthy NLP, including explainability, factuality, and accountable reasoning for tasks such as fact checking and question answering. She develops systematic methods to test and improve the faithfulness of model explanations and LLM-driven verification. Her PhD dissertation received awards from ELLIS and Informatics Europe, and she serves as Industry Track Chair for EMNLP 2026.
keynote.speaker3Affiliation: Hassan II University of Casablanca, FSBM
keynote.speaker3Bio: Professor at the Faculty of Sciences Ben M'Sik, Hassan II University of Casablanca. Coordinates innovation and technology transfer initiatives, including the Technology Innovation Support Center (TISC). Contributes to research and teaching in entrepreneurship, innovation, and intellectual property, and is actively involved in organizing IANLP 2026.
keynote.speaker4Affiliation: University of Michigan, Computer Science & Engineering
keynote.speaker4Bio: Janice M. Jenkins Collegiate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at the University of Michigan, where she directs the Artificial Intelligence Laboratory and the Language and Information Technologies (LIT) Lab. Her research spans natural language processing, multimodal processing, and computational social science. Co-inventor of the TextRank algorithm. Fellow of the ACM, AAAI, and ACL, and former president of the Association for Computational Linguistics.
keynote.speaker5Affiliation: LTIM, Hassan II University of Casablanca, FSBM
keynote.speaker5Bio: Professor at the Laboratory of Information Technology and Modeling (LTIM), Faculty of Sciences Ben M'Sik, Hassan II University of Casablanca. Her research focuses on artificial intelligence, machine learning, IoT systems, and intelligent applications in education and healthcare.

venue.aboutCasablancaDesc: Morocco's largest city and economic hub, known for its modern architecture, vibrant culture, and rich history.
venue.gettingThereDesc: Casablanca is well connected through Mohammed V International Airport (CMN), located approximately 30km south of the city center. Regular flights connect to major European and international hubs.
venue.accommodationDesc: Casablanca offers a wide range of hotels and accommodations. Additional information about recommended venues and special conference rates will be announced soon.
venue.moreInfoDesc: Detailed travel guides, visa requirements, local attractions, and dining recommendations will be provided in the conference portal.
venue.mapDesc: Interactive map and detailed venue information coming soon. Maps and directions will be available in the conference registration portal.

textToVideo.title: From Text to Video: Foundations, Challenges & AI Video Generation
textToVideo.subtitle: Practical Workshop 2 — IANLP 2026
textToVideo.description: An introduction to text-to-video generation — exploring core principles, technical challenges, and current applications in education, communication, media, and content creation.
textToVideo.animatorRole: PhD candidate, Faculty of Sciences Ben M'Sick, Hassan II University of Casablanca
textToVideo.program2: How generative AI video systems work
textToVideo.program3: Hands-on experimentation with video generation tools
textToVideo.program4: Analyzing results and discussing current limitations
textToVideo.audience: Students, PhD candidates, researchers, teachers, professionals, entrepreneurs, and anyone interested in AI video content creation.

noCodeAutomation.title: No-Code AI Automation Lab: Intelligent Workflows with n8n & Google Opal
noCodeAutomation.subtitle: Practical Workshop — IANLP 2026
noCodeAutomation.description: Learn to design, automate, and deploy intelligent no-code workflows using n8n, Google Opal, and generative AI — for scientific monitoring, document summarization, file classification, report generation, and smart assistants.
noCodeAutomation.animatorRole: PhD in AI — Data Scientist, AI and IT Professional Trainer
noCodeAutomation.program2: Introduction to n8n and Google Opal
noCodeAutomation.program4: Use cases for education, research, business, and healthcare
noCodeAutomation.audience: Students, PhD candidates, teachers, researchers, engineers, professionals, and anyone interested in no-code intelligent automation.
